sutta: then the householder anata pindika with about 500 lay disciples around him came to the exalted 1 saluted or paid homage and sat down at 1 side and the exalted 1 spoke to him so seated and said verily householder you have served the sangha of monks with gifts of the requisites the robe arms lodging and medicine for sickness but you must not be satisfied just with by the thought we have served the sangha with gifts of the requisites why householder or therefore householder train yourself thus come now let us from time to time enter and abide in the delight pt that comes of seclusion verily train yourself thus householder [Music] and when he had thus spoken the variable sarikuta said to the exalted 1 it is marvelous lord it is wonderful how well the exalted 1 has spoken lord when the aryan disciple enters and abides in the delight that comes of seclusion for him 5 things become not for him at that time the pain and grief that follow lust become not the pleasure and gratification that follow lust become not the pain and grief that follow evil become not the pleasure and gratification that follow evil become not for him at that time the pain and grief that followed doing good become not lord when the aryan disciple enters and abides in the delight pity and that comes of seclusion for him these 5 things become not it is even as you say when sarikuta the aryan disciple enters and abides in the delight that comes of seclusion for him at that time these 5 things become not as the end of the suta
commentary: here uh the buddha is giving very good advice to the anatapindika pindika is his greatest layman supporter who built the large jaitavana monastery at savathi for the buddha when you go to india you can find that monastery the ruins are still there supposed to go about about 25 acres and this natha pindika was a very rich man but his pen it seems he spent all his fortune supporting the monks he's reported to give food to 2000 monks every day and here the buddha is telling him this is not enough to do dhana it shouldn't from time to time meditate and then attain that pity and the delight of seclusion and the reason for this is uh because because there are 2 kinds of merit and doing charity or offerings is worldly merit and although it supports the spiritual life by itself it doesn't get us out of suffering doesn't get us out of the wheel of existence a wheel of rebirth and to get out of the wheel of ebird we need a spiritual merit and spiritual marriedness [Music] is because all the things in the are in eightfold paths but uh uh the most important uh would be meditation in meditation we train our mind we develop our mind to see things and more clearly and that helps us not only this life also it helps us in the next life if we develop our mind then we go to a place where the mind of those beings are more developed that's why there is an episode in the mahayana tradition about the um indian monk bodhi dharma 1 of the earliest monks who went to china he was supposed to have been invited by the emperor to the palace and the emperor told the variable buddhi dharma that he had done a lot of charity he had built a lot of monasteries and nunneries and did a lot of charity and he said to the verbal bodhi dharma that he should have obtained a lot of merit and the verbal bodhi dharma said you have obtained no marriage something like that and the king was very annoyed and refused to speak to verbal bodhi dharma after that so bodhidharma left the palace and later he learned from his advisor that the bodhi dharma was a very holy person and whatever bodhidharma said must be true and then he tried to get the advisor to call bodhidharma to come back to the palace but the buddhi the advisor said once he decided to go nobody can call him back so what the bodhi dharma meant he was because the king asked whether he had this refers more to spiritual marriage and bulidama sin by doing all that charity he had 0 0 spiritual merit what he meant was that the emperor had a lot of food footer is worldly married not a spiritual married [Music] now the next suta is

pleasure and gratification that follow lust

pleasure and gratification that follow evil
